_note: Node-local MEASURE-plane config (RP-0010 Plane 1). Copy to a local, gitignored path (e.g. /var/lib/mycelium/measure.config.json) and pass it via 'myceliumd --measure-config' ALONGSIDE --reachability-config (the plane folds the reachability snapshot). Each member's 'ref' MUST match a reachability-config target 'ref' (the join key) — the daemon REFUSES TO START if a member has no matching reach target (an unmeasured member would stay seeded-clean and never be rotated away from). 'tick_ms' MUST be >= the slowest member's reach probe interval (else the daemon refuses to start) — a tick that outpaces the probes re-folds the same window and defeats the detector anti-flap / over-reinforces the tuner. 'proto' is a closed-registry transport id (control/vocab.json); its class is derived from it. 'action' is the move that promotes the member when it is a candidate (promote-sibling / rotate-port / regen-reality). 'active_ref' is the currently-active member and is re-read each tick; it must be one of the members. The daemon writes the assembled rotate.PlanInput to 'output_path' (atomically) — that file feeds 'myceliumctl rotate-plan'. The PlanInput's 'now' is its freshness stamp: across failing ticks the file FREEZES at the last good tick, so the consuming loop MUST reject a PlanInput whose 'now' is stale (older than ~2*tick_ms). This config drives NO actuation; rotation stays behind the RP-0012 triple gate. With no config supplied, the plane does not run.
